root_motivation: Turn 'handles avoid the LLM-as-calculator problem' into a measurable number by comparing value-error rates between arm A (model computes from raw rows) and arm B (server computes via dataset_* tools).
five_whys: mqo-vs-sql-bench measures query construction but not answer-value correctness — the calculator failure mode is invisible without this benchmark.
Value-error rate (arithmetic/transcription/wrong-subset) is the precise metric: arm B should approach zero because the server computed every reported value.
Two-arm head-to-head design produces a signed delta, making the improvement claim falsifiable.
Configurable shell-out grader reuses the mqo-vs-sql-bench grading convention and allows real graders without binary changes.
Fixture + stub-grader path ensures cargo test passes in CI with no live cluster or API key.

acceptance_criteria.text: Run both arms over a tasks file and produce a per-task result table with each arm's reported answer and error classification.
Aggregate reports value-error rate (overall and by class), retries, latency, and token deltas between arms.
Equivalence/correctness judged by the configurable shell-out grader, not bespoke compare; the grader command is a config field.
The report names the winning arm per metric and writes both JSON and markdown.
Runs green end-to-end on a bundled fixture (recorded arm outputs + stub grader); no live cluster, model key, or external grader install required.
cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings clean.

hard_constraints: Only deps: serde_json, clap, thiserror; grader/dh-mcp-server invoked as external commands
No live cluster or model API key required for cargo test
keystone_deps: dh-mcp-server (https://github.com/joeyen-atscale/dh-mcp-server) — invoked as external command, not a Cargo dep
